title: salix subnivalis
intro: Salix subnivalis, also known as the Himalayan willow, is a species of willow tree that is native to the Himalayan region and is known for its ability to thrive in high-altitude environments. The bark and leaves of Salix subnivalis have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Salix subnivalis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
